overview.service: Cloud infrastructure, enterprise database systems, and electronic health record platforms
overview.consumer_base: Global health systems, government agencies, and enterprise organizations
overview.partnership_rationale: Oracle Health (formerly Cerner) is one of the two dominant EHR vendors in the US, and Oracle's cloud infrastructure serves major federal health agencies including the VA and DoD, making it a high-value partner for UNC's clinical informatics and health data interoperability research.
overview.contact_reasoning: Oracle operates a formal academic research program providing cloud credits and technical collaboration to universities, with health and life sciences as a stated priority area.

unc_connection.donation_history: No confirmed direct donation history to UNC on record. Verify via UNC Office of Development.

talking_points.detail: Oracle Health (Cerner) is deployed across hundreds of US health systems including major academic medical centers and federal health programs (VA, DoD), making interoperability research with Oracle's platform directly relevant to multi-site clinical data studies.

Oracle holds the contract for the US Department of Veterans Affairs EHR modernization, one of the largest health IT projects in US history, generating research opportunities around large-scale health data migration and interoperability.

Oracle for Research provides free cloud credits to academic institutions for high-performance computing, data analytics, and AI workloads — applicable to UNC's genomics pipelines, clinical AI research, and population health modeling.

Oracle Health has invested heavily in FHIR R4 compliance and health data interoperability standards, creating alignment with UNC's NC TraCS Institute work on standardized clinical research data exchange.

Oracle Health manages one of the largest clinical data repositories in the world through its Cerner Real-World Data platform, which is accessible to academic researchers for retrospective studies and ML model development.

references.title: Oracle Health — EHR and Clinical Solutions
